msgs.text:
According to the current attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 117 is again an attachment-only continuation. I used only the three files attached to this message.

Turn 117 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state remains unchanged from Turn 116:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The original problem is still exactly the over-G isomorphism question: whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH such thatπH∘Φ=πS.This is the question stated in the prompt and problem statement.The attached sweep’s theorem coordinate remains stronger than the quotient-level statement: it records a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G⟶F=P⋊GwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,i.e.ΓS≅GΓH.

This is exactly the desired condition πH∘Φ=πS.No structural computation should be reopened under the current inputs. The frozen proof chain remains:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+ Certificates D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The history also makes clear why this is not a case to keep hammering: the non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the Cm=Lm induction; the coefficient tower closes after exact normalization to the row (2,t) and the Koszul--Witt cascade; and the finite filtered Nielsen compactness construction globalizes the finite solutions to the over-G automorphism.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments are still only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger. They do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D artifacts: no D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs independent of the history ledger. Therefore the strict gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. History-recorded verifier claims support the theorem ledger, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict Certificate D gate.

So the attachment-only transition rule remains idempotent:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 117 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 116. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN117-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
